spec.doc: StarTrack Label Specifications - Modify Your Own System (MOS) v9

rules.title: StarTrack label dimensions
rules.description: Despatch labels are 100 x 150 mm (optionally 100 x 200 mm with the top 150 mm reserved for StarTrack); controlled returns/transfer labels are 150 x 100 mm.

messages.pass: Page dimensions match an accepted StarTrack label size ({actual}).
messages.fail: Page dimensions do not match the accepted StarTrack label sizes ({actual}).
messages.missing: Physical dimensions could not be determined. Review whether the label is 100 x 150 mm, 100 x 200 mm or 150 x 100 mm.

rules.title: StarTrack logo/header
rules.description: The P-StarTrack logo must appear on the left side of the label header.

messages.fail: StarTrack logo/header may be image-only or was not extracted as text. Confirm on the preview.

rules.title: Label code displayed in header
rules.description: The 3-character label code for the StarTrack product must appear above the logo (Premium: white on black; Express/Special Services: black on white).

messages.missing: A three-character label code was not extracted from the text layer or barcode data.

rules.title: Connote number displayed in header
rules.description: The consignment number must appear in the centre of the header under a CONNOTE: heading, catering for up to 20 characters.

messages.missing: CONNOTE value was not extracted from the text layer or decoded barcode payload.

rules.title: Receiver details present
rules.description: Receiver name, address, suburb, state and postcode must be displayed beneath the logo.

messages.pass: Receiver details were extracted from the text layer or decoded QR payload.
messages.missing: Receiver details could not be isolated from the text layer or decoded QR payload.

rules.title: Sender details present
rules.description: Sender name, phone and address must be displayed beneath the routing barcode.

messages.missing: Sender details could not be isolated from text.

rules.title: Weight displayed
rules.description: The consignment weight in kg must appear under the WEIGHT heading.

messages.missing: Weight value was not extracted from the text layer or decoded barcode payload.

rules.title: Cubic volume displayed
rules.description: The consignment cubic volume in cubic metres must appear under the CUBE heading.

messages.missing: Cubic volume was not extracted from the text layer or decoded barcode payload.

rules.title: StarTrack 2D QR barcode decoded
rules.description: The StarTrack 2D QR barcode must appear on all labels and carry the fixed-width shipment payload.

messages.pass: StarTrack QR payload decoded from the uploaded file.

rules.title: Source image resolution
rules.description: Raster label images need roughly 300 DPI for reliable linear barcode decoding. Low-resolution exports collapse the narrow bars and spaces, destroying the encoded data.

messages.pass: Estimated image resolution is about {actual} DPI, which should be sufficient for barcode decoding.
messages.fail: Estimated image resolution is about {actual} DPI (assuming a standard 100mm label edge). Linear barcodes are typically unrecoverable below about 200 DPI - upload the original PDF or export the label at 300 DPI or higher.

rules.title: Freight item barcode decoded
rules.description: The 20-character StarTrack Code 128 freight item barcode must be present on every article.

messages.pass: StarTrack Code 128 freight item barcode(s) decoded.

rules.title: Routing barcode decoded
rules.description: An approved sortation barcode must be present: the StarTrack routing barcode, or the GS1 421 routing barcode for SSCC labels.

args.label: A StarTrack routing barcode (or GS1 421 routing barcode)

rules.title: Freight barcode despatch ID
rules.description: The freight item barcode must start with the 4-character alphanumeric despatch ID issued by StarTrack.

rules.title: Connote sequence
rules.description: Characters 5-12 of the freight barcode are the 8-digit consignment sequence.

rules.title: Freight item sequence
rules.description: The final 5 characters of the freight barcode are the numeric item sequence within the consignment.

rules.title: Freight barcode product code
rules.description: Characters 13-15 of the freight barcode must be a valid StarTrack product code.

rules.title: Code 128 compression structure
rules.description: The freight item barcode must use mixed Code 128 character sets: the first 4 characters in Code B (despatch ID), the next 8 in Code C (consignment sequence), the next 4 in Code B (product code + first item digit) and the final 4 in Code C. The decoded text must therefore read as 4 alphanumeric + 8 digits + 3 alphanumeric + 5 digits. The physical subset switching inside the symbol can only be confirmed with a barcode verifier at print time.

messages.pass: Decoded freight barcode structure is consistent with the required Code B/C compression pattern: {actual}.
messages.fail: Decoded freight barcode structure does not match the Code B/C compression pattern (4 alphanumeric + 8 digits + 3 alphanumeric + 5 digits): {actual}.

rules.title: Product matches the audited label type
rules.description: The product code in the freight barcode must belong to the product family selected for this audit.

rules.title: Visible connote matches freight barcode
rules.description: The CONNOTE value printed in the header must equal the connote embedded in the freight item barcode.

rules.title: Routing label code known
rules.description: The first three characters of the routing barcode must be a valid StarTrack label code.

rules.title: Routing postcode
rules.description: Characters 4-7 of the routing barcode must be the four-digit receiver postcode (9901 for NZ Premium).

rules.title: Routing and product compatibility
rules.description: The routing label code must correspond to the product on the freight barcode or QR payload (for example FPP routes as PRM, FPA routes as ARL).

rules.title: Routing postcode matches QR postcode
rules.description: The postcode in the routing barcode must equal the receiver postcode in the QR payload.

rules.title: Code 128 compression structure
rules.description: The routing barcode must use mixed Code 128 character sets: the first 3 characters in Code B (label code), the next 4 in Code C (receiver postcode) and the remaining 2-3 characters in Code B (depot or primary port). The decoded text must therefore read as 3 alphanumeric + 4 digits + 2-3 alphanumeric. The physical subset switching inside the symbol can only be confirmed with a barcode verifier at print time. The GS1 421 routing form used on SSCC labels has its own structure and is exempt.

messages.pass: Decoded routing barcode structure is consistent with the required Code B/C compression pattern: {actual}.
messages.fail: Decoded routing barcode structure does not match the Code B/C compression pattern (3 alphanumeric + 4 digits + 2-3 alphanumeric): {actual}.

rules.title: QR mandatory fields populated
rules.description: All mandatory fixed-width QR fields must be populated: receiver suburb/postcode/name/address, connote, freight item, product, quantity, weight, despatch date, unit type, destination depot, DG indicator and movement type.

rules.title: QR receiver postcode
rules.description: QR field 2 must be a four-digit postcode (9901 for NZ Premium).

rules.title: QR consignment number format
rules.description: QR field 3 must follow the connote format: 4-character despatch ID plus 8 digits.

rules.title: QR freight item number format
rules.description: QR field 4 must be the 20-character freight item identifier: despatch ID + 8-digit connote sequence + 3-character product + 5-digit item sequence.

rules.title: QR product code known
rules.description: QR field 5 must be a valid StarTrack product code.

rules.title: QR product matches the audited label type
rules.description: The QR product code must belong to the product family selected for this audit.

rules.title: QR consignment quantity
rules.description: QR field 8 must be a numeric consignment quantity of at least 1.

rules.title: QR consignment weight
rules.description: QR field 9 must be the total consignment weight in whole kilograms, rounded up.

rules.title: QR despatch date
rules.description: QR field 11 must be a valid calendar date in YYYYMMDD format.

rules.title: QR unit type permitted
rules.description: QR field 14 must be an Appendix A unit type permitted for the product code.

rules.title: QR dangerous goods indicator
rules.description: QR field 19 must be Y or N.

rules.title: QR movement type indicator
rules.description: QR field 20 must be N (despatch), C (controlled return) or T (transfer).

rules.title: QR book-in window dates
rules.description: When populated, the not-before date must be YYYYMMDDHHMM and must not exceed the not-after date.

rules.title: QR not-after date format
rules.description: When populated, the not-after date must be a valid YYYYMMDDHHMM value.

rules.title: QR ATL number format
rules.description: When populated, QR field 23 must use the C999999999 ATL format.

rules.title: RA number required for returns and transfers
rules.description: QR field 24 (RA/TA number) is mandatory when the movement type is C (controlled return) or T (transfer).

messages.pass: RA/TA number is populated for this return/transfer movement: {actual}.
messages.fail: Movement type indicates a controlled return or transfer, but the RA/TA number field is blank.
messages.missing: Movement type indicates a controlled return or transfer, but the RA/TA number field is blank.

rules.title: Return/transfer indicator displayed
rules.description: Controlled returns and transfer movements must display a *RETURN* or *TRANSFER* indicator in the centre of the label header.

messages.pass: Return/transfer indicator found on the label: {actual}.
messages.missing: QR movement type indicates a return/transfer, but no *RETURN* or *TRANSFER* indicator was extracted from the label text.

rules.title: ATL barcode present when expected
rules.description: When the label or QR payload indicates Authority To Leave, the C999999999 ATL barcode must be printed in the header.

messages.fail: Authority To Leave is indicated on the label, but no C999999999 ATL barcode was decoded.

rules.title: ATL sequential counter
rules.description: The ATL barcode counter must start from 000000001.

rules.title: ATL barcode matches QR/visible ATL number
rules.description: The decoded ATL barcode must equal the ATL number carried in the QR payload and any visible ATL text.

rules.title: Code 128 compression structure
rules.description: The ATL barcode must use mixed Code 128 character sets: the first 2 characters in Code B (the literal C plus the first counter digit) and the remaining 8 in Code C. The decoded text must therefore read as the literal C followed by 9 digits. The physical subset switching inside the symbol can only be confirmed with a barcode verifier at print time.

messages.pass: Decoded ATL barcode structure is consistent with the required Code B/C compression pattern: {actual}.
messages.fail: Decoded ATL barcode structure does not match the Code B/C compression pattern (literal C + 9 digits): {actual}.

rules.title: QR connote matches freight barcode
rules.description: The consignment number in the QR payload must equal the connote embedded in the freight item barcode.

rules.title: QR freight item matches freight barcode
rules.description: The freight item number in the QR payload must equal the decoded 20-character freight item barcode.
